content.text: The Team Plan is Claude's collaborative tier for groups of five or more people who need a shared AI workspace rather than individual subscriptions. It sits between the single-user Pro plan and the contract-based Enterprise plan, providing team administration, shared Projects, higher usage limits, and a default no-training policy without requiring a dedicated procurement process.

Understanding the Team Plan means understanding three things: what you get that Pro does not provide, what the admin controls actually look like in practice, and where the hard stops are that push organisations toward Enterprise.

attrs.alt: Claude Team Plan capabilities compared to Free and Pro tiers

content.text: The comparison above highlights the key differentiators: shared Projects that persist across sessions, central seat management with one billing account, access to all current models including Opus 4, and a default position that conversation data is not used for model training.
Core Capabilities
Shared Projects

Projects are the central collaboration primitive in the Team Plan. A Project is a persistent workspace that holds a system-level custom instruction, a set of knowledge files, and a running conversation history. Every team member who is added to a Project sees the same instruction and files, and any conversation they start within that Project inherits that context automatically.
Custom instructions: a markdown system prompt set by the Project owner, applied to every conversation inside that Project. Used for persona, output format, or domain-specific ground rules.
Knowledge files: uploaded documents, spreadsheets, or code files the Project owner attaches. Claude reads these on every turn — no manual re-upload per conversation.
Shared visibility: all workspace members with access to the Project can see and continue each other's conversations, creating a shared memory of decisions and outputs.
Artifacts: code blocks, documents, and tables generated inside a Project can be saved as Artifacts — reusable outputs that persist across sessions and are visible to the team.
Model Access

Team seats receive access to the full current Claude model family. At the time of writing that includes Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Haiku 4.5, and Claude Opus 4.8. The Pro plan provides the same model access, but the Team Plan bundles that access for every seat under one subscription.

Model selection is per-conversation: a member can choose a faster Haiku for routine summaries and switch to Opus 4.8 for complex reasoning tasks. There is no admin control over which models individual members use — that level of restriction is an Enterprise feature.
Usage Limits

The Team Plan provides higher usage limits per seat than Pro. Anthropic does not publish exact message counts for either plan; the documented guarantee is that Team usage limits are higher than Pro. In practice, most teams do not hit the daily limit on normal workloads.

When a seat approaches its limit, Claude does not stop responding. It continues but may use a lower-tier model or shorter context window to manage load. The member sees a soft warning. Hard caps are not enforced in the same way that API rate limits are — the system degrades gracefully rather than returning 429 errors.
No-Training Default

By default, conversations on Team accounts are not used to train Claude models. This is the opposite default from Free accounts (where training opt-out requires deliberate action). Team Plan members do not need to toggle any privacy setting — the no-training guarantee is enforced at the billing tier level, not per-user preference.

Data privacy note: the Team Plan no-training guarantee applies to conversation content. Account metadata (billing, usage counts, error logs) is handled under Anthropic's standard privacy policy regardless of plan.
Administration
Organisation Structure

A Team subscription maps to one Organisation in claude.ai. An Organisation has one billing account, one or more Admins, and any number of Members up to the seat limit purchased. Workspaces within the organisation (in the Cowork product) are a separate concept — the basic Team Plan exposes a single shared workspace.
Roles
Role
Can do
Cannot do
Admin
Invite/remove members, view billing, change org settings, create/delete shared Projects, promote Members to Admin
Cannot restrict which models members use; cannot set per-user message quotas; no audit log access
Member
Create personal Projects, join shared Projects they are added to, use all models, view and continue shared conversations they have access to
Cannot invite new members, cannot view billing, cannot delete other members' conversations
Inviting and Removing Members

Admins invite members by email address from the Organisation settings page. An invitation email is sent; the recipient accepts and their seat activates. Billing begins immediately on acceptance. Removing a member deactivates their seat; prorated credit is applied to the next billing cycle.

There is no bulk-import CSV flow at the Team tier — invitations are sent one at a time or in a small batch through the UI. Domain-level auto-provisioning (where anyone with a company email joins automatically) is an Enterprise feature requiring SSO configuration.
Billing

The Team Plan is priced per seat per month. Annual prepay is typically offered at a discount. Billing is to a single credit card or via invoice for larger accounts (though invoice billing at Team tier is subject to minimum seat counts that vary by region).

Adding seats mid-cycle results in a prorated charge for the remaining days. Removing seats produces a prorated credit. The billing admin receives a monthly receipt by email; no per-seat usage breakdown is included — only total seat count and total charge.

attrs.alt: Team Plan admin seat management workflow and key feature areas

content.text: Setting Up a Shared Project
This is the most common admin task after inviting members. A shared Project takes five minutes to configure correctly.
Navigate to claude.ai and open the Projects sidebar. Click New Project.
Give the Project a name that describes its purpose (e.g. 'Customer Support — Tier 1 Triage').
Open Project Settings. Paste a custom instruction. Be specific: describe the persona, output format, and any hard rules Claude should follow for every conversation in this Project.
Upload knowledge files if the team has reference material Claude should always have — a style guide, product FAQ, codebase README, or regulatory checklist.
In the Sharing section, select 'Everyone in workspace' or add specific members. Members added here will see this Project appear in their sidebar.
Verify by starting a test conversation inside the Project. Confirm the custom instruction applies and knowledge files are indexed.
Writing Effective Custom Instructions

Custom instructions in a Team Project function like a system prompt injected before every user turn. The quality of the instruction directly determines how consistently Claude behaves across different members' conversations.
Be explicit about format:
'Always respond with a numbered list of action items followed by a one-paragraph summary.' is more reliable than 'Be concise.'
Include negative constraints:
'Do not recommend specific legal entities or give tax advice.' prevents common liability-adjacent outputs.
Name the persona:
'You are a senior software reviewer for a Python-first team. Default to PEP 8 style guidance.' helps Claude stay on topic across members.
Reference the knowledge files:
If you have uploaded a product FAQ, tell Claude in the instruction: 'When answering product questions, check the FAQ file first before generating from memory.'
Keep it under ~2000 words:
Very long custom instructions consume context window budget on every turn, reducing how much content a member can include in a single conversation.
Team Plan vs Enterprise Plan

The Team Plan is self-serve: any individual can sign up, add a credit card, and have a team workspace in minutes. Enterprise requires a sales conversation, contract negotiation, and minimum seat counts. The feature difference that most often drives the decision is SSO and audit logs.
Capability
Team Plan
Enterprise Plan
Minimum seats
Typically 40+ (varies by contract)
Self-serve signup
Yes — immediate
No — sales process required
SSO / SAML
Not available
Required / available
SCIM provisioning
Not available
Available (auto-provision/deprovision)
Audit logs
Not available
Available (who did what, when)
Per-member usage controls
Not available
Available
Data residency options
Not available
Available (US, EU, APAC)
Dedicated support
Standard support only
Named CSM, priority SLA
Custom model fine-tuning
Not available
Available (with Anthropic partnership)
Deployment flexibility
Claude.ai web only
API + claude.ai + custom integrations

The decision rule of thumb: if your IT team requires SSO for compliance, or if your security team needs an audit trail of AI interactions, you need Enterprise. If neither of those applies and your team is 5–40 people who primarily work through the web interface, Team is sufficient.
Common Operational Questions
What happens when someone leaves the company?

An Admin removes the departing member from the Organisation settings. Their seat deactivates immediately. Their personal Projects are deleted (since those belong to their account). Conversations they contributed to inside shared Projects remain visible to the team — they are not deleted when a member is removed. This is important for continuity: the team retains access to prior outputs.
Can team members see each other's personal conversations?

No. Personal Projects and standalone conversations (those not inside a shared Project) are only visible to their owner. Admins do not have a view into member-private conversations. Shared visibility is limited to conversations created inside shared Projects that the member has been granted access to.
Can we use the Team Plan with the Claude API?

The Team Plan is a claude.ai subscription, not an API tier. It does not grant API keys or access to the Anthropic API. If your team needs programmatic access (to build integrations, pipelines, or agents), you need a separate Anthropic API account billed by token usage. The two billing tracks are independent.
How do usage limits interact with shared Projects?

Each message in a shared Project is attributed to the member who sent it. Their individual seat's usage counter is what decrements. There is no shared pool across the team. If a high-volume member hits their daily limit, they degrade gracefully while other members' limits are unaffected.
Migrating from Individual Pro to Team Plan
When a team outgrows individual Pro subscriptions, the migration process requires care to preserve conversation history and avoid double-billing during the transition.
Migration Steps
Have a designated admin create the Organisation under a single email address (typically a team alias like ai-tools@company.com, not a personal address).
Add a payment method to the Organisation billing account. Do not cancel individual Pro subscriptions yet.
Set the Team Plan to the desired seat count. Purchase seats equal to the number of people migrating.
Invite each team member by their work email. Confirm they accept and can access the Team workspace.
Have each member export any critical conversation history from their personal Pro account. There is no automated migration — personal conversations from Pro accounts do not transfer to Team Projects.
After confirming all members are active on Team, cancel each individual Pro subscription. Pro subscriptions bill to the end of their current cycle; no prorated refund is issued.
Set up shared Projects to recreate any shared knowledge (system prompts, reference docs) that team members previously duplicated in their individual accounts.

The most common mistake is cancelling Pro subscriptions before verifying that the Team account is working correctly for all members. Run a parallel period of one week where both plans are active; the overlap cost is small relative to the risk of a member losing access.
What Doesn't Transfer
Personal conversation history: conversations in a Pro account stay in that account. They are not visible to the Team organisation and cannot be bulk-imported.
Personal custom instructions: any custom instructions a member set on their individual account do not appear in the Team workspace. They must be re-entered in Team Project instructions.
Uploaded files from personal sessions: files uploaded to a personal Pro conversation are not accessible in Team Projects. Re-upload relevant files as Project knowledge files.
Pro billing credits: if a member had unused Pro days at cancellation, these are not credited to the Team account. They are applied to the personal account's final billing cycle only.
Structuring a Team Workspace for Scale

A common mistake is creating one large shared Project for the entire team. As the team grows and use cases multiply, a single Project becomes cluttered — the custom instruction tries to serve too many purposes and the conversation history becomes hard to navigate.
Project Taxonomy Patterns
Organise Projects by job function or workflow, not by topic. Each Project should have a clear owner who maintains the custom instruction and knowledge files.
Pattern
Example Projects
Works best when
By team function
Engineering Code Review, Marketing Copy, Legal Triage, Customer Support
Teams have distinct workflows with different output formats and domain knowledge
By workflow stage
Research, Drafting, Review, Publishing
Content or document workflows where same team handles multiple stages
By client or product
Client A, Client B, Internal Product
Agencies or teams managing separate accounts that must not share context
By urgency tier
Async Research, Real-time Support
Teams with both high-latency and low-latency AI tasks using different Claude models
Knowledge File Best Practices
Each Team Project supports knowledge file uploads that Claude reads on every turn. Choosing the right files and keeping them current is an ongoing admin responsibility.
Upload reference, not output:
Style guides, regulatory checklists, product specifications, and schema documentation belong in knowledge files. Draft outputs, which change frequently, do not.
Use plain text or markdown where possible:
Claude reads PDF and DOCX but extracts text only. Formatting-heavy files lose their structure. Convert tables to markdown before uploading if the table structure matters.
Version control knowledge files externally:
Knowledge files cannot be versioned inside claude.ai. Keep canonical copies in a Git repository or document system; re-upload when they change.
File count and size limits:
Each Project supports up to 20 knowledge files with a combined size limit (typically 10 MB per file, 200 MB per Project). Exceed this by splitting large references or summarising verbose documents.
Announce changes to the team:
When a knowledge file is updated, team members do not receive a notification. Send a Slack message or email to let the team know what changed and why.
Security Considerations for Teams

The Team Plan provides meaningful security improvements over individual Pro accounts, but it does not include the full enterprise security surface. Knowing where the gaps are is essential for teams in regulated industries.
What the Team Plan Provides
No model training on conversation data by default — this is the most important security property for teams handling sensitive business information.
Central seat management — when an employee leaves, a single admin action deactivates their access. No need to coordinate password resets across individual accounts.
HTTPS in transit for all web traffic — standard TLS for the claude.ai web interface.
Anthropic's standard data handling and retention policies — conversations are retained for service improvement purposes under the stated retention windows in the privacy policy.
What the Team Plan Does Not Provide

SSO / SAML — members authenticate via personal email/password or Google OAuth, not via your corporate identity provider. An employee who leaves but retains access to their personal Google account can still log in until an admin removes their seat manually.

Audit logs — there is no record of which member ran which conversation, which Project they accessed, or what outputs were generated. If your security policy requires this for compliance, you need Enterprise.

Data residency guarantees — data may be processed on Anthropic's standard infrastructure without a contractual data-residency commitment. EU teams with GDPR requirements should verify with Anthropic's sales team whether Team Plan terms satisfy their DPA obligations.
IP allowlisting — you cannot restrict access to claude.ai by source IP address at the Team tier.
Zero-data retention mode — available at Enterprise, this configuration causes Anthropic to not log conversation content at all, even transiently. Team Plan does not offer this.

Compliance guidance: if your team is subject to HIPAA, SOC 2, or FedRAMP requirements, consult with Anthropic's enterprise sales team before deploying the Team Plan for workflows involving regulated data. These frameworks typically require BAAs, audit trails, or data-residency terms that are only available at Enterprise.
Practical Security Hygiene for Team Admins
Audit seat list monthly: export the member list from Organisation Settings and compare against your HR system. Remove seats for departed employees within 24 hours of offboarding.
Require two-factor authentication on member personal accounts: Team Plan cannot enforce 2FA centrally, but you can make it a policy requirement and request members verify they have it enabled.
Do not upload PII or credentials to knowledge files: knowledge files are shared with everyone in the Project. Avoid uploading files that contain personal data, API keys, or database credentials.
Use purpose-specific Projects to contain data scope: instead of one Project with access to all company knowledge, create narrow Projects where members only see the knowledge they need.
Rotate admin access: when an admin leaves, immediately remove their admin role and promote another member. Billing account access does not transfer automatically.
Troubleshooting Common Issues
Member cannot see the shared Project

Check three things in order: (1) the member has accepted their Organisation invitation — pending invitations do not grant Project access; (2) the Project's sharing setting includes 'Everyone in workspace' or the member's email specifically; (3) the member is looking at the correct workspace — if they have both a personal account and the Team workspace, they may be viewing the wrong one.
Custom instructions are not being applied

Custom instructions apply only to conversations started inside the Project. If a member starts a new conversation from the main sidebar (outside any Project), the Team Project's instructions are not in scope. Verify the member is clicking New Chat from within the Project, not from the global sidebar.
Billing shows more seats than expected

Each accepted invitation creates one active seat, even if the member uses the account infrequently. Accepted invitations that were intended as testing should be removed. Pending (not yet accepted) invitations do not create a billable seat — billing starts on the moment of acceptance.
Knowledge files are not being referenced by Claude

Large knowledge files with complex formatting may be improperly parsed. Test by uploading a plain text version of the same content and comparing Claude's responses. Also check the file count — Projects support up to 20 files. If the Project has hit its limit, older files may be deprioritised. Confirm by removing a file and re-uploading the one that seems unread.
Summary

The Team Plan provides persistent shared Projects, access to all Claude models, higher per-seat usage limits, and a default no-training policy under one centralised billing account starting at five seats. Administration is lightweight — Admins invite and remove members and manage shared Projects through a standard UI, with no SSO or audit capability. For teams that need identity federation, compliance audit logs, or API access, Enterprise is the next tier. For teams of 5–40 people working through the claude.ai interface without those compliance requirements, the Team Plan is the right level of service.
